title: Failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Attempting to create and run custom containers.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pod create --name postgres --network mynetwork --share net

2. podman run --name=postgres-1 -d --pod=postgres -v $SCRIPT_DIRECTORY/postgres/:/etc/postgres-init/:Z $REPO_URL/postgres:2022.02.1

**Describe the results you received:**
`
ERRO[0000] Starting some container dependencies
ERRO[0000] "failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory
`

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect the pod to be created and the containers to be started successfully.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
slirp4netns is installed as I saw that was the solution for most

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client: Podman Engine
Version: 4.0.2
API Version: 4.0.2
Go Version: go1.17.7

Built: Tue Apr 19 06:16:32 2022
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Running on Red Hat 8 on vSphere 6.7

comments.body: Does it work when run a simple container: `podman run --network podman alpine ip a`?

It does not. The same error is thrown.

Error: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory
Is `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` env var set correctly? Did it worked with previous versions? Can you that command with `--log-level debug` and share the output.

`echo $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` yielded the output `/run/user/1000`
Command with the --log-level debug output is:

```
Error: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory
```

Some additional information, as I am seeing this same problem.

I'm running a fresh RHEL 8.6 off the RHEL ISO using the BaseOS and AppStream repos. I've run the following since the machine finished it's install.
```
yum install @container-tools
usermod --add-subuids 100000-165535 --add-subgids 100000-165535 dewthefifth
dnf install podman-plugins
```

I followed the instructions at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md to validate that slirp4netns and fuse-overlay are both installed.

I'm able to run my nginx image using `podman run -it --entrypoint /bin/bash $IMAGE`. However, attempting to run my nginx image using `podman run -it --entrypoint /bin/bash --network podman $IMAGE`. Produces the same errors described by this ticket/issue. 

Mostly commenting from a reproducability perspective.

Can you run `podman unshare --rootles-netns`, I assume that this will also fail. If this is the case please run `strace -f podman unshare --rootles-netns > strace.log` and upload the log here so that I can take a look.

Please `rm -rf /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/rootless-netns` and `rm /run/user/1000/netns/rootless-netns-b1bdf11f0614475dd17b` and try again. Or you could just reboot. It looks like the state got messed up somehow.

That fixed it! Any idea what might've caused that? All that has been executed on this machine is the commands stated above (installing necessary dependencies and then the podman commands).

Thanks for the help!

The solution did not work for me, but I have resolved the issue.

After removing the /run/usr/1000 files I was getting the following (even after a restart):
```
> podman network create test-network
> podman run -it --network test-network --entrypoint /bin/bash $IMAGE
Error: open /etc/resolv.conf: no such file or directory
> podman run -it --network test-network --entrypoint /bin/bash mvn.cubrc.org:8444/common/nginx:2022.04.2
Error: failed to mount runtime directory for rootless netns: no such file or directory
```

I checked, and the machine (VM) this is running on and confirmed that it did not have a /etc/resolv.conf. From what I can tell this was likely caused by the fact that the VM is operating with a host only adapter that I've been manually enabling on each start. 

Running `systemctl reload NetworkManager` created an /etc/resolv.conf file, but I was still in the failed mount runtime directory state. I had to delete the /run/usr/1000 after the /etc/resolv.conf had been created before I was able to start containers as expected. 

Hopefully this information is helpful.

I am not sure how you end up in such state but I think that the rootless netns setup is not as robust as it should be. The main problem is that if for any reason an error occurs we do not cleanup the netns.
